title: Panic acute stress and medical red flag check
category: Medical and first · Aid · Disaster · Health
summary: Medical-heavy survival entry for calming support while checking for chest pain, breathing danger, injury, substances, or self-harm risk. It is designed for offline emergencies and does not replace professional care.
content: Use this entry to organize calming support while checking for chest pain, breathing danger, injury, substances, or self-harm risk. Start with scene safety, hand hygiene, calm communication, and written timing. Keep the person comfortable, protect privacy, avoid risky experiments, and prepare a clear handover for a doctor, ambulance, clinic, pharmacist, or trained first-aider. Watch especially for: chest pain, fainting, confusion, intoxication, injury, suicidal talk, or cannot calm after danger ends. In a long emergency, review the person regularly instead of assuming they are stable.
materials: clean water if available. Context: “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”.
soap or sanitizer if available — for «Panic acute stress and medical red flag check»
gloves or clean barrier if available — for «Panic acute stress and medical red flag check»
paper and pen. Context: “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”.
phone or radio if available — for «Panic acute stress and medical red flag check»
labels for times and medicines — for «Panic acute stress and medical red flag check»
steps: Check that the place is safe for the helper and the injured or sick person. Apply it specifically within the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” procedure.
Call emergency help or send a reliable person for help when any red flag is present. Apply it specifically within the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” procedure.
Write the time the problem started, what changed, medicines taken, allergies, and known conditions. Apply it specifically within the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” procedure.

Use clean hands and clean barriers before touching wounds, body fluids, food, medicines, infants, or medical devices. Apply it specifically within the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” procedure.
Keep the person warm or cool as appropriate, still if trauma is possible, and protected from crowds. Apply it specifically within the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” procedure.
Recheck and record changes regularly so deterioration is noticed early. Apply it specifically within the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” procedure.

Prepare a short handover note with name, age, symptoms, times, medicines, allergies, and actions already taken. Apply it specifically within the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” procedure.
warnings: Do not use this as a replacement for training, emergency services, a clinician, or local medical guidance. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”.

Do not guess medicine doses, mix medicines without label checking, share prescriptions, or perform invasive procedures. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”.
Escalate urgently if you see: chest pain, fainting, confusion, intoxication, injury, suicidal talk, or cannot calm after danger ends.

Keep children, elders, pregnancy, disability, immune weakness, and chronic illness in the high-risk group. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”.
common_mistakes: Waiting too long after red flags appear while carrying out “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
Not writing times and medicines while carrying out “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
Touching wounds or fluids without hand hygiene while carrying out “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
Letting crowds block air, privacy, or transport while carrying out “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
Assuming a quiet person is stable without rechecking while carrying out “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
alternatives: Use official first-aid training when available as a fallback for “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
Use local emergency numbers and pharmacists for medicine questions as a fallback for “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
Use paper handover notes when phones are dead as a fallback for “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
Use a trusted translator if language blocks care as a fallback for “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
failure_signs: Red flags appear: chest pain, fainting, confusion, intoxication, injury, suicidal talk, or cannot calm after danger ends
The person becomes harder to wake, breathe, drink, speak, walk, or recognize during “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
Pain, bleeding, swelling, fever, confusion, or weakness gets worse during “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check”
when_not_to_use: Do not delay emergency care to complete the checklist when using the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” record
Do not move a trauma patient unless staying is more dangerous when using the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” record
Do not force care on a conscious person except immediate life safety situations under local rules when using the “Panic acute stress and medical red flag check” record
short_term: Stabilize the scene, reduce immediate harm, record key facts, and connect the person to trained help. This note applies specifically to Panic acute stress and medical red flag check.
long_term: Keep medical records updated, train the group in certified first aid, maintain supplies, and practice respectful handovers. This note applies specifically to Panic acute stress and medical red flag check.
if_method_fails: Escalate to emergency services, nearest clinic, pharmacist, radio medical consult, or evacuation route; document the failed step and time. This note applies specifically to Panic acute stress and medical red flag check.
environment_notes: In apartments, heat, smoke, poor ventilation, crowds, no elevator, and water outages can make medical problems worse. Plan quiet space, clean water, light, and transport. This note applies specifically to Panic acute stress and medical red flag check.

title: Self harm risk immediate safety plan
category: Medical and first · Aid · Disaster · Health
summary: Medical-heavy survival entry for staying with the person, reducing immediate danger, contacting trusted help, and emergency services. It is designed for offline emergencies and does not replace professional care.
